purpose: Maintain sourced academic next-app baseline numbers without treating incompatible datasets, splits, or metric definitions as direct DiPECS comparisons.

dipecs_reference.metric_scope: micro hit@K and MRR@5 from the committed DiPECS LSApp Standard report

comparability_legend.direct: Same dataset, same split construction, same metric definition, and same candidate/label pipeline as DiPECS.
comparability_legend.contextual_only: Useful literature context, but at least one of dataset, preprocessing, split, candidate set, or metric definition differs from DiPECS.
comparability_legend.excluded_unclear: The source does not expose enough compatible Top-K detail for a numeric DiPECS comparison.

baselines.metric_scope: micro hit@K and MRR@5 from the committed DiPECS LSApp Standard report
baselines.comparability_note: Same JSON report, same Standard split, same test windows, and same metric implementation as the DiPECS ensemble row.

baselines.paper: Mobile App Prediction Leveraging Large Language Model Embeddings
baselines.split: standard, chronological 70/10/20 by user in the TGT reproduction table
baselines.metric_scope: HR@K and MRR@K reported as percentages; MAPLE values are cited by TGT from the original paper without standard deviations.
baselines.comparability_note: LSApp is shared, but DiPECS uses its own committed report/candidate pipeline and does not reproduce MAPLE preprocessing, train/validation handling, or model artifacts.
baselines.source_locator: Table II, LSapp standard split, MAPLE row

baselines.paper: Mobile App Prediction Leveraging Large Language Model Embeddings
baselines.split: cold-start, user-level 90/10 in the TGT reproduction table
baselines.metric_scope: HR@K and MRR@K reported as percentages; MAPLE values are cited by TGT from the original paper without standard deviations.
baselines.comparability_note: Cold-start evaluates unseen users and is not the same split objective as the DiPECS Standard report.
baselines.source_locator: Table III, LSapp cold-start split, MAPLE row

baselines.paper: TGT: A Temporal Gating Transformer for Smartphone App Usage Prediction
baselines.metric_scope: HR@K and MRR@K reported as mean percentages over 5 runs.
baselines.comparability_note: Same public dataset family and Top-K vocabulary, but not the same DiPECS trace conversion, candidate filtering, report fixture, or deployment constraints.
baselines.source_locator: Table II, LSapp standard split, TGT row

baselines.paper: TGT: A Temporal Gating Transformer for Smartphone App Usage Prediction
baselines.metric_scope: HR@K and MRR@K reported as mean percentages over 5 runs.
baselines.comparability_note: Cold-start evaluates unseen users and cannot be read as a direct DiPECS Standard split comparison.
baselines.source_locator: Table III, LSapp cold-start split, TGT row

baselines.paper: Appformer: A Novel Framework for Mobile App Usage Prediction Leveraging Progressive Multi-Modal Data Fusion and Feature Extraction
baselines.dataset: Shanghai carrier app usage with POI features
baselines.split: paper-specific partition based on PAULCI comparison
baselines.metric_scope: Hit@K and MRR reported by AppFormer Table V; MRR is reported as average reciprocal rank rather than a DiPECS MRR@5 fixture.
baselines.comparability_note: Different dataset, POI feature source, partitioning scheme, and MRR definition; useful only as a multimodal literature reference.
baselines.source_locator: Table V, Appformer + K + T row

baselines.paper: Appformer: A Novel Framework for Mobile App Usage Prediction Leveraging Progressive Multi-Modal Data Fusion and Feature Extraction
baselines.dataset: Shanghai carrier app usage with POI features
baselines.split: paper-specific partition based on DUGN comparison
baselines.metric_scope: Hit@K, NDCG@K, and MRR@K reported by AppFormer Table VI; Hit@1/3/5 are extracted for the DiPECS matrix.
baselines.comparability_note: Different dataset and split; the row helps position AppFormer but cannot establish a direct DiPECS win/loss.
baselines.source_locator: Table VI, Appformer + K + T row

baselines.paper: POI-aware / location-context app usage baseline as cited by AppFormer
baselines.dataset: Shanghai carrier app usage with POI features
baselines.split: paper-specific partition based on AppFormer Table V
baselines.metric_scope: Hit@K and MRR reported by AppFormer Table V.
baselines.comparability_note: Different source dataset and feature assumptions; retained because issue #102 explicitly asks for POI-style app prediction context.

baselines.paper: Smartphone App Usage Prediction Using Points of Interest
baselines.split: paper-specific sparse sampling and location-based popularity estimation
baselines.metric_scope: Top-5 hit rate for popular applications at a location, not next individual app prediction.

raw_reported_metrics.name: location_top5_popular_app_hit_rate_pct
raw_reported_metrics.scope: location-level top-five popular app hit rate

baselines.comparability_note: The reported 83.0% hit rate targets top-five popular apps by location, so it is not a DiPECS next-app window metric.

baselines.paper: Next app prediction based on graph neural networks and self-attention enhancement
baselines.split: first five days train, sixth day validation, seventh day test
baselines.metric_scope: TopK Hitrate, TopK MRR, and TopK NDCG with K=1 and K=5.
baselines.comparability_note: The article page documents the dataset, split, and metrics, but the numeric table is not encoded until the exact values are re-verified from an accessible table.
baselines.source_locator: Datasets, Experiment metrics, Results and analysis

baselines.paper: Historical app prediction family, exact paper mapping pending
baselines.metric_scope: No sourced Top-1/Top-3/Top-5 row has been verified for the current DiPECS fixture.
baselines.comparability_note: Kept as an explicit maintenance placeholder because issue #102 names APPredict/HAPP-style baselines, but no exact compatible numeric claim is recorded without a primary source.
